QF.id: VB Legal Structure of Mutual Funds_8

answer.text: Association of Mutual Funds in India
Association of Market and Financial Institutions
Audit and Monitoring of Financial Investments

explanation.text: **The Logic:** AMFI stands for Association of Mutual Funds in India. It is the industry association of mutual funds in India. Note: AMFI is neither a regulatory body nor a Self-Regulatory Organisation (SRO). All AMCs are members of AMFI. AMFI works to promote the mutual fund industry, protect investor interests, set ethical standards, and register mutual fund distributors. **The Trap:** AMFI is NOT a regulator (SEBI is the regulator) — it is an industry association. Think of it as a professional body that sets standards and promotes best practices. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under industry bodies. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** AMFI is important for you because it is the body that registers mutual fund distributors and issues ARN (AMFI Registration Number). Without an ARN from AMFI, you cannot sell mutual funds.
